headline: Walmart Is Focused on Faster Delivery, and It’s Opening Special New Stores to Prove It
summary: Walmart is opening "dark stores" that serve solely as fulfillment centers to speed up online order deliveries, aiming to reach 95% of the U.S. population with three-hour or less delivery. This initiative, alongside expanded drone delivery services, is part of Walmart's strategy to enhance delivery speed and compete with other retail giants. These stores are not accessible to the public and are designed internally to resemble regular Walmart outlets, stocking popular products for efficient online order processing.
label: positive

headline: Home Depot makes an acquisition, MillerKnoll announces tariff-related losses, and more
summary: This article provides a weekly digest of design and business news, highlighting several major developments. Home Depot's subsidiary SRS Distribution is acquiring GMS Inc. for $5.5 billion, strengthening its position in the pro customer segment. MillerKnoll reported a significant net loss of $36.9 million for fiscal year 2025 due to tariffs and high tax rates, despite a slight increase in net sales. Additionally, Parachute is closing 19 stores to refocus on partnerships and core product categories, and Swedish textile recycler Syre is partnering with Gap and Target to provide recycled polyester.
label: negative

headline: Walmart installing EV chargers at Jacksonville stores
summary: Walmart is moving forward with plans to install electric vehicle (EV) chargers at three Supercenter locations in Jacksonville, Florida, with each site costing $1.3 million. This initiative is part of Walmart's broader goal, announced in April 2023, to establish its own EV fast-charging network at thousands of its stores nationwide by 2030, supplementing existing third-party chargers. The company aims to make EV ownership more accessible by leveraging its widespread presence, providing convenient charging options in various communities.
label: negative

headline: Kirkland's Inc. Accelerates New Era with Strategic Leadership Appointments to Advance Transformation as The Brand House Collective
summary: Kirkland's Inc. announced a series of strategic leadership appointments to advance its transformation into The Brand House Collective, a multi-brand merchandising, supply chain, and retail operator. Andrea Courtois has been named SVP, Chief Financial Officer, while Michael Sheridan joined as SVP, General Counsel & Corporate Secretary, and Mandy Gauldin was promoted to VP, Talent & Culture. These changes reflect the company's commitment to building a disciplined, performance-led, multi-brand operating model to achieve profitable growth and enhance its portfolio of home and family brands.
label: negative

headline: Congressional Black Caucus left unimpressed after 'candid' meeting with Target CEO amid sustained boycott
summary: The Congressional Black Caucus (CBC) recently met with Target's CEO to discuss the company's rollback of diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) policies amidst an ongoing nationwide boycott. Despite a "candid" conversation, the CBC expressed disappointment with Target's explanations, stating they fell short of what their communities deserve. The caucus urged Target to publicly recommit to DEI values, reinstate initiatives, update on financial investments, and disclose workforce and supplier diversity data to regain trust and address the significant financial toll already taken due to the boycott.
label: neutral

headline: Target tests smartphone tech to unlock anti-teft cases - Bizwomen
summary: Target Corporation is testing smartphone-enabled technology to replace traditional keyed locks on anti-theft cabinets, following similar moves by Walmart and CVS. While currently only Target staff are using the digital unlocking system, the goal is to extend this feature to Target Circle loyalty program members and Shipt delivery shoppers. This initiative is part of Target's ongoing efforts to reduce "inventory shrink" caused by shoplifting, complementing other measures like self-checkout limits and specialized cameras.
label: positive

headline: Walmart testing new 'dark stores' that won't be open to the public
summary: Walmart is testing new "dark stores" that will operate similarly to traditional Walmart stores but will not be open to the public. These facilities are designed to stock popular online order items to speed up fulfillment and enhance competition with Amazon. Walmart also plans to test smaller distribution centers for customers willing to pay extra for merchandise.
label: positive

headline: Walmart tests dark stores
summary: Walmart is piloting "dark stores" in Dallas and Bentonville, Arkansas, brick-and-mortar locations that fulfill online orders but are not open to the public. This initiative aims to accelerate and streamline online fulfillment, contributing to Walmart U.S. achieving e-commerce profitability in Q1 for the first time with sales up 21%. The company expects to reach 95% of the U.S. population with delivery options of three hours or less soon.
label: negative

headline: The Weekly Closeout: J. Jill names chief merchant as Spirit Halloween resurrects seasonal hiring
summary: J. Jill appointed Courtney O’Connor as its new chief merchandising officer, bringing 20 years of retail experience, while Spirit Halloween plans to hire 50,000 seasonal employees. Nike added former Lego CEO Jørgen Vig Knudstorp to its board as it implements a turnaround strategy. Meanwhile, Goop is expanding its brick-and-mortar presence, and Claire's is reportedly exploring a potential sale due to accumulating financial difficulties.
label: negative

headline: America’s Car-Mart Appoints New Chief Financial Officer and Senior Vice President of Colonial Finance
summary: America's Car-Mart, a leading automotive retailer, has announced two key executive appointments: Jonathan Collins as Chief Financial Officer and Marie Persichetti as Senior Vice President of Colonial Auto Finance. Collins brings extensive global finance experience from Walmart, while Persichetti has a strong background in financial leadership from companies like Bayside Credit and AutoNation. The appointments aim to strengthen the company's financial and automotive financing leadership.
label: positive

headline: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Ray Dalio, Warren Buffett, Dan Loeb, Balyasny Asset Management, Intergroup Corp (INTG), Walmart Inc (WMT), and More
summary: This article compiles recent hedge fund and insider trading news involving prominent investors like Ray Dalio, Warren Buffett, and Dan Loeb. It covers various investment strategies, stock picks, and significant insider transactions, including Walmart's $329 million stock sale by the Walton family and details on Balyasny Asset Management and Intergroup Corp (INTG). The article also highlights insights into market trends, AI stock performance, and hedge fund activities.
label: positive

headline: Walmart tests dark stores to accelerate delivery
summary: Walmart is piloting dark stores, which are brick-and-mortar locations not open to the public, to fulfill online orders and speed up delivery. The retailer is testing the concept in Dallas with plans for another in Bentonville, Arkansas, as part of its strategy to enhance e-commerce operations and achieve faster delivery times. This initiative follows Walmart U.S. achieving e-commerce profitability in Q1, driven by sales growth and declining net delivery costs.
label: negative

headline: Walmart Taps VERB's MARKET.live Platform for Exclusive BelliWelli Mystery Product Launch
summary: VERB Technology's MARKET.live division will produce and host an exclusive livestream shopping event for gut-health brand BelliWelli on Walmart.com on July 1, 2025, at 4:00 PM PT. The event, featuring BelliWelli's CEO Katie Wilson, highlights VERB's growing presence in interactive video social commerce and represents a collaboration with Walmart's traditional livestream producer, TalkShop Live. This partnership underscores the increasing trend of major retailers utilizing livestreaming to enhance customer engagement and conversion rates.
label: negative

headline: Starbucks Elects Dambisa Moyo and Marissa Mayer to its Board of Directors
summary: Starbucks announced the election of Dr. Dambisa Moyo and Marissa Mayer to its Board of Directors, effective June 25, 2025. Both Moyo, a global economist, and Mayer, a technology leader, bring extensive experience in finance, technology, and global affairs. Their appointments are expected to support Starbucks' "Back to Starbucks" strategy, focusing on innovation and customer experience.
label: positive

headline: Best Buy divests home care firm Current Health
summary: Best Buy has divested its home care firm, Current Health, selling it back to co-founder Christopher McGhee. The electronics retailer had originally acquired Current Health in 2021 for approximately $400 million, but its health unit faced headwinds, including a significant goodwill impairment charge. McGhee plans to rebuild Current Health, asserting that the future of healthcare lies in home and community-based services, while Best Buy continues to manage its Lively senior support brand and emergency response devices.
label: positive

headline: Walmart testing ‘dark stores’ to fill online orders, according to report
summary: Walmart is reportedly testing "dark stores" in the Dallas area and Bentonville, Ark., to expedite online order fulfillment, a strategy similar to one Amazon has employed for years. These dark stores function as micro-distribution centers, stocking popular online items for immediate delivery and are closed to the public. This initiative is part of Walmart's broader efforts to speed up delivery through various technologies and services, including drone delivery, advanced geospatial technology, and same-day pharmacy delivery.
label: positive

headline: Meatpacking giant JBS debuts on NYSE six months after $5m Trump donation
summary: Meatpacking giant JBS debuted on the NYSE after a nearly two-year delay and a $5 million donation from its subsidiary to Donald Trump's 2025 inauguration. Environmental groups and a U.S. Senator raised concerns about a potential quid-pro-quo given JBS's history of corruption and environmental degradation. The company defended the donation as part of its civic engagement.
label: neutral

headline: Prime Time For Amazon? JPMorgan Says Your $139 Could Be Worth $1,430
summary: JPMorgan is bullish on Amazon (AMZN) despite a stock drag in 2025, with analyst Doug Anmuth setting a $240 price target, driven by the increasing value of its Prime subscription. The report estimates Prime's annual value at $1,430, over ten times its $139 cost, due to enhanced services and faster fulfillment. A projected Prime price hike in 2026 is expected to boost sales without significant churn, fueled by growing global subscribers.
label: negative

headline: Walmart to test dark stores in Bentonville, Dallas metro
summary: Walmart is testing "dark stores" (online fulfillment centers not open to the public) in Dallas and Bentonville to enhance online order delivery, particularly for groceries. This initiative follows Walmart's investment in automated micro-fulfillment centers and aims to improve delivery speed and efficiency for its e-commerce business. The strategy is designed to address issues like store aisle congestion and maintain market share, with a goal to deliver to 95% of U.S. shoppers within three hours by year-end.
label: negative

headline: Walmart Tests Dark Stores in its Multi-Front Effort to Improve Delivery
summary: Walmart is reportedly testing "dark stores" (mini-warehouses not open to the public) in Dallas and Bentonville, Arkansas, to improve delivery speed and reach. This initiative is part of a broader strategy that also includes expanding InHome delivery, integrating prescriptions into delivery options, deploying geospatial technology to expand delivery areas, and broadening drone delivery services to more cities. The retail giant aims to support a wider delivery radius and faster deliveries by stocking popular products in these dark stores.
label: negative
